task.summary: Evaluate cross-model advisory output after the owner clarified that the chatbot demo must be static/rule-based and AI should be framed as an adjacent possibility, with emphasis on security, data protection, and time/money savings.

model_or_worker.name: Codex GPT agents, Claude CLI, Gemini CLI, and Decision Mesh
model_or_worker.role: cross-model examples and chatbot positioning advisory

reasoning_profile.change_policy: Use as advisory positioning only; implementation still requires an approved spec and local verification.

token_efficiency_profile.context_scope: Redacted examples strategy, previous agent notes, static chatbot constraint, and compact Decision Mesh guidance.

input_packet_summary.summary: A bounded Radeq examples packet covering the owner correction, prior GPT agent findings, Claude/Gemini advisory prompts, and the no-LLM static chatbot constraint.
allowed_context: static chatbot and email handoff constraint

output_pointer.summary: Accepted synthesis: first wave should use a static chatbot/pruvodce demo, automation and e-shop offer examples, while AI is described as an optional light extension and data safety is a repeated trust layer.

prompt_or_input_delta.summary: The input changed from AI-helper/RAG-like wording to a static chatbot/pruvodce with prepared rules, small database, and explicit email handoff.
prompt_or_input_delta.rollback_path: Remove this eval and work-log entry if the owner decides to ship a model-backed assistant instead.

verification_evidence.evidence: Codex GPT agents, Claude CLI, and Gemini CLI converged that the helper should be bounded and that AI language must not overpromise a static rule-based demo.
Decision Mesh selected external advisory review and bot/data/security constraints, with redacted context only.
Claude and Gemini CLI availability was verified; Gemini required a .cmd wrapper because PowerShell script execution was blocked.
